chief_complaint: A 10-year-old girl presented with abdominal symptoms, ascites, and pleural effusion.
history: The patient was initially diagnosed with abdominal symptoms, ascites, pleural effusion, panniculitis, and arthritis. She was ultimately diagnosed with chronic pancreatitis with associated PPP syndrome.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Imaging studies and blood tests revealed systemic fat necrosis and pancreatic enzyme leakage.
correct_diagnosis: Chronic pancreatitis with associated PPP syndrome
differentials: Chronic pancreatitis with associated PPP syndrome
correct_ranking: Chronic pancreatitis with associated PPP syndrome
